goal: Record a 45-second 1080p clip of the running demo via Isaac Sim's built-in capture, not OS screen-record.
thoughts: record_demo_video gives him clean viewport-only output -- no Dock, no Slack notifications, no browser tab. Set 1080p, 45s. Confirm sim is in Play state before starting. If capture fails for whatever reason, stills via capture_viewport are a valid fallback. No OBS / ffmpeg rabbit-hole for a 45-second clip.
code:
state = get_timeline_state()
if not state.is_playing:
    sim_control(action="play")
record_demo_video(
    output_path="<OUT_MP4>",
    duration_seconds=45,
    camera_path="<CAMERA_PRIM_OR_NONE_FOR_ACTIVE>",
    fps=30,
    width=1920, height=1080,
    include_ui=False
)
# Fallback if record_demo_video fails:
# capture_viewport(path="<OUT_DIR>/frame_%04d.png", width=1920, height=1080)
failure_modes: Sending him to install OBS or ffmpeg for a 45-second viewport clip
Recording with include_ui=True so the play button and Slack notifications are in frame
Starting capture before sim Play so the robot is frozen in the video
